card.intuition: Managing changes to your API so that existing clients don't break when you introduce brand-new features or structural changes.
card.analogy: A physical library: instead of rearranging all the books every time a new category is added, you build an 'Extension' (v2) and keep the old rooms (v1) exactly as they are so regular visitors don't get lost.
when_to_use: When making 'breaking changes' (removing fields, changing data types, changing endpoints)
Maintaining legacy support for old mobile app versions that can't be updated instantly
Providing a 'stable' vs 'experimental' interface for developers
gotchas: Try to avoid versioning for as long as possible (additive changes are NOT breaking changes)
API versioning in the URL (e.g., /v1/users) is the most common, but versioning via Headers (Accept: application/vnd.v2+json) is considered more RESTful

steps.annotation: System overview — step through to trace the flow.
Client specifies version via URL path, header, or query param.
Router detects version and dispatches to correct handler.
v2 handler returns new schema without breaking old clients.

exercise.prompt: Identify the three main ways to version an API.
hints: Query params and Headers use the version number or string
exercise.test_code:
assert "v1" in versioning_methods[0]
assert "Header" in versioning_methods[2]
print("PASS")
